title: Carpotroche brasiliensis
taxonomy: Carpotroche brasiliensis is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant belongs to the order Malpighiales and the subclass Rosidae. It is a part of the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae. The family Phyllanthaceae is a part of the larger clade that includes other flowering plants. Carpotroche brasiliensis is a species of flowering plant native to South America.
uses: Carpotroche brasiliensis has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties. In traditional medicine, Carpotroche brasiliensis is believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating skin infections and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Carpotroche brasiliensis.

The health benefits of Carpotroche brasiliensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
